title: What to know about Angelica sinensis before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Angelica sinensis are treating anemia due to its rich iron content and antioxidant properties, which help increase red blood cell count and reduce oxidative stress. Additionally, it is used to alleviate fatigue and digestive issues, such as constipation and bloating, thanks to its prebiotic and anti-inflammatory properties, which promote gut health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Angelica sinensis is also employed to treat menstrual disorders, including heavy menstrual bleeding and irregular periods, as it has been shown to decrease uterine contractions and improve estrogen levels. Furthermore, its anxiolytic and bronchodilatory properties make it a useful treatment for anxiety and asthma, reducing symptoms of anxiety and improving lung function. By modulating the body's hormonal response, Angelica sinensis has been found to relieve menstrual cramps, hypertension, diarrhea, and menopause symptoms, including hot flashes and night sweats.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Angelica sinensis are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which has been linked to the presence of volatile oils and saponins that inhibit the growth of pathogens and bacteria. The antioxidant properties of this plant are due to the flavonoids and phenolic acids that scavenge free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage, thus contributing to its anti-inflammatory effects, which are mediated by the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Additionally, Angelica sinensis exhibits analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which are thought to be related to the presence of sesquiterpenes and alkaloids that modulate pain and smooth muscle contraction. The antipyretic effects of this plant are also noteworthy, and are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes that reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis has been shown to possess anticancer, immunomodulatory, cardioprotective, and neuroprotective properties, which are thought to be related to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that inhibit cancer cell growth, modulate the immune response, protect the cardiovascular system, and prevent neurodegenerative diseases.
identification: Angelica sinensis is a perennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, characterized by its large, coarse, and hairy leaves, which are pinnate with 5-7 pairs of leaflets and a long petiole. It typically grows to a height of 1.5-2.5 meters, with a stout stem and a large, hollow, and grooved root. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of China, specifically in the Sichuan, Guizhou, and Yunnan provinces, where it thrives in humid, subtropical climates at elevations between 1,500 and 3,000 meters above sea level. The plant's inflorescence is a large, compound umbel, composed of numerous small, yellowish-green flowers. It is often found in association with other plants, such as Astragalus and Gentiana, in the mountainous forests and meadows of its native range.
cultivation: Angelica sinensis is a perennial plant native to China, which typically thrives in growth habits that include a tall stem (up to 2 meters) and a clump-forming habit. This plant requires well-drained soil with a pH range of 6.0-7.0 and adequate moisture, but can tolerate some drought. Optimal growth conditions include partial shade and temperatures between 15-25°C, although it can also grow in full sun and tolerate temperatures up to 30°C. To cultivate Angelica sinensis successfully, it is essential to plant the rhizomes in early spring or autumn, and to maintain a consistent moisture level, as it is susceptible to overwatering. Regular harvesting of the foliage can also help promote healthy growth and prevent the plant from flowering prematurely.
history: Angelica sinensis is a medicinal plant that originated in China, where it has been used for over 2,000 years to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its use was documented in the Shennong Bencaojing, a classic Chinese medical text, where it was described as having a "calming heat" effect. Traditionally, the plant was used to treat menstrual disorders, reduce inflammation, and improve circulation, with its root considered the most medicinal part. In traditional Chinese medicine, Angelica sinensis is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects, and it is still commonly used today in the form of teas, tinctures, and capsules. Modern studies have confirmed the plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, supporting its traditional uses in treating conditions such as arthritis and menopausal symptoms.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids in Angelica sinensis possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, such as flavonoids, ferulic acid, and sinapic acid.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: A type of glycoside found in Angelica sinensis, characterized by its surface-active and foaming properties, along with its ability to bind to cholesterol and form complexes with it.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a group of medicinal constituents in Angelica sinensis with anticoagulant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Ligustilide
constituents.description: Ligustilide is a sesquiterpene lactone constituent of Angelica sinensis, known for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Lignans
constituents.description: Lignans are a type of polyphenolic compound found in Angelica sinensis, characterized by their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Terpenoids from Angelica sinensis are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Polysaccharides
constituents.description: Polysaccharides from Angelica sinensis are large molecular weight, water-soluble, and have immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Butylphthalide
constituents.description: Butylphthalide is a sesquiterpene lactone with neuroprotective, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory properties.

constituents.name: Furanocoumarins
constituents.description: Furanocoumarins are a class of compound found in Angelica sinensis, exhibiting photochemical and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Berberine, a specific alkaloid in Angelica sinensis, has antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and anti-diabetic properties.

constituents.name: Ferulic acid
constituents.description: A phenolic compound with antioxidant properties, free radical scavenging activity, and anti-inflammatory effects.

constituents.name: Essential oils
constituents.description: Angelica sinensis essential oils have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Volatile oils
constituents.description: Angelica sinensis volatile oils are reported to have a sweet and aromatic scent, and contain compounds such as ligustilide and butylphthalide.

constituents.name: Coumarin glycosides
constituents.description: Coumarin glycosides are known to have anticoagulant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties

constituents.name: Phytosterols
constituents.description: Phytosterols are a type of plant sterols that are known for their ability to lower cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation.

constituents.name: Acidic Glycosides
constituents.description: Acidic Glycosides are bioactive compounds found in Angelica sinensis, characterized by their acidic and glycosidic properties.

constituents.name: Phthalides
constituents.description: Phthalides are sesquiterpene lactones found in Angelica sinensis with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Iridoid glycoside
constituents.description: Iridoid glycoside is a compound found in Angelica sinensis, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Coumarinolignans
constituents.description: Coumarinolignans are a type of phenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and pro-apoptotic properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Abdominal pain occurs in some individuals due to gastrointestinal irritation caused by Angelica sinensis.
Acute urticaria, angioedema, and anaphylaxis occur due to IgE-mediated hypersensitivity.
Angelica sinensis can cause abnormal heart rhythms due to its effect on the heart's electrical conduction system, which is mediated through its component ligustilide.
A decrease in heart rate caused by excessive use of Angelica sinensis.
Excessive coughing occurs in some individuals who ingest Angelica sinensis due to the plant's irritating saponins.
High doses of Angelica sinensis cause gastrointestinal irritation leading to loose stools.
Excessive use of Angelica sinensis causes headache in some individuals due to its coumarin and ligustilide content.
Dose-dependent decrease in blood pressure has been observed
Angelica sinensis ingestion causes stomach discomfort resulting in nausea.
Excessive consumption of Angelica sinensis causes vomiting in some individuals

intro_description: Angelica sinensis, commonly known as Chinese Angelica, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions, including menstrual disorders, rheumatism, and high blood pressure. Its main medicinal uses include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and relieving symptoms of menopause. The herb's main health benefits include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immune-boosting properties. Its main therapeutic properties include analgesic, anti-spasmodic, and anti-arrhythmic effects. The herb's main bioactive compounds include ferulic acid, ligustilide, and butylphthalide, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chinese Angelica is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and is a key ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine.

uses_list.description: This plant is used for hypertension because it is believed to have a hypotensive effect, which is the ability to lower blood pressure, due to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content.

This plant is used for menstrual disorders because it is thought to have a balancing effect on the menstrual cycle, and its antispasmodic properties may help to alleviate cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.

This plant is used for diarrhea because it is believed to have a stabilizing effect on the intestinal tract, and its flavonoids and phenolic acids may help to reduce inflammation and improve intestinal health.

This plant is used for asthma because it is thought to have a bronchodilatory effect, which is the ability to open up airways, and its flavonoids and phenolic acids may help to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health.

This plant is used for rheumatoid arthritis because it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to reduce joint inflammation and improve symptoms associated with the condition.

This plant is used for anemia because it is thought to have a stimulating effect on the production of red blood cells, and its iron content may help to alleviate symptoms associated with iron deficiency anemia.

This plant is used for digestive issues because it is believed to have a soothing effect on the digestive tract, and its flavonoids and phenolic acids may help to reduce inflammation and improve intestinal health.

This plant is used for fatigue because it is thought to have a stimulating effect on the body, and its flavonoids and phenolic acids may help to improve energy levels and reduce feelings of exhaustion.

This plant is used for rheumatism because it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to reduce joint inflammation and improve symptoms associated with the condition.

This plant is used for cough because it is thought to have a expectorant effect, which is the ability to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, and its flavonoids and phenolic acids may help to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Angelica sinensis is used to treat asthma by inhibiting bronchial inflammation and improving lung function. Research has also shown that its flavonoids and phenolic acids can help manage diabetes by reducing blood glucose levels and improving insulin sensitivity. Additionally, the plant is used to alleviate symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis through its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to inhibit pro-inflammatory cytokines. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis is used to treat hypertension due to its vasodilatory effects and ability to reduce blood pressure. It is also used to address menstrual disorders, cancer prevention, anemia, menstrual cramps, hepatitis, high blood pressure, chronic fatigue, menopausal symptoms, fatigue, inflammation, diarrhea, cardiovascular disease, gastroenteritis, menstrual pain, bronchitis, and digestive issues through its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Angelica sinensis is used to treat hypertension by reducing blood pressure and relieving stress on the cardiovascular system. It is also used to alleviate diarrhea, digestive issues, and fatigue by regulating the body's internal balance and improving nutrient absorption. Additionally, Angelica sinensis is employed to treat menstrual disorders, including menstrual cramps and pain, by regulating the menstrual cycle and alleviating symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances. Furthermore, it is used to address cough, inflammation, asthma, anemia, anxiety, hepatitis, rheumatism, and epilepsy by modulating the body's immune response and regulating various physiological processes. Moreover, Angelica sinensis is used to treat arthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, cardiovascular disease, cancer, and menstrual disorders by inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and reducing inflammation in the body.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Angelica sinensis is used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, which its flavonoids and phenolic acids help to regulate, as well as digestive issues, such as diarrhea, by modulating gut motility and secretion. Asthma patients may benefit from its anti-inflammatory properties, while rheumatoid arthritis sufferers can take advantage of its immunomodulatory effects and anti-inflammatory activity, which also helps alleviate menstrual disorders. Additionally, Angelica sinensis is used to treat anxiety by influencing the nervous system, anemia through its iron content and anti-inflammatory properties, and rheumatism by reducing inflammation and improving joint function. Furthermore, it is used to alleviate fatigue, eczema through its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and cough due to its expectorant properties, as well as diabetes by enhancing insulin sensitivity and glucose uptake in the cells. Its anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties also make it a treatment option for menstrual cramps, migraines, chronic fatigue syndrome, inflammation, menopause symptoms, epilepsy, and gastroesophageal reflux.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Angelica sinensis is used to treat menstrual disorders, such as dysmenorrhea, by regulating the menstrual cycle and relieving symptoms. It is also employed to alleviate gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea, by reducing inflammation and promoting digestion. Furthermore, the plant is utilized to manage respiratory conditions, including bronchitis and cough, as well as asthma, through its expectorant and bronchodilatory properties. Additionally, Angelica sinensis is used to treat cardiovascular disease, hypertension, and anemia by improving circulation and reducing blood pressure. It is also effective in treating rheumatoid arthritis, rheumatism, and arthritis, as well as inflammation, digestive issues, gastroenteritis, kidney stones, hemorrhoids, respiratory infections, and chronic fatigue.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Angelica sinensis is used to treat a range of health conditions, including asthma, which it helps alleviate through its expectorant properties, as well as hypertension, a condition it addresses by promoting vasodilation and improving cardiovascular function. It is also employed to manage rheumatoid arthritis, a chronic inflammatory condition characterized by joint pain and swelling, by reducing inflammation and improving joint mobility. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis is used to treat anemia, a condition characterized by a deficiency in red blood cells or hemoglobin, by increasing red blood cell count and improving oxygen delivery to tissues, as well as diarrhea, a condition it addresses by regulating bowel function and improving gut health. Additionally, it is used to treat rheumatism, a condition characterized by joint pain and stiffness, and menstrual cramps, a condition it addresses by relaxing uterine muscles and improving menstrual flow. The plant is also used to treat cough, fatigue, menstrual pain, digestive issues, bronchitis, migraines, menorrhagia, high blood pressure, diabetes, gastritis, nausea, hemorrhoids, and menstrual disorders, making it a versatile treatment option in homeopathic medicine.

benefits_list.description: This plant has antioxidant properties due to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including ferulic acid, caffeic acid, and volatile oils, which are capable of scavenging free radicals and neutralizing oxidative stress, thereby protecting cells from damage and contributing to overall health and well-being.

This plant has anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as ferulic acid, caffeic acid, and volatile oils, which are capable of inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating associated symptoms.

This plant has been found to have a hypotensive effect, which is attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as ferulic acid, caffeic acid, and volatile oils, which are capable of relaxing blood vessels, improving blood flow, and reducing blood pressure.

This plant has been found to have neuroprotective effects, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as ferulic acid, caffeic acid, and volatile oils, which are capable of scavenging free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and improving cognitive function by enhancing memory, attention, and learning ability.

This plant has been traditionally used to relieve menstrual pain and other associated symptoms, such as cramps, bloating, and mood swings, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.

This plant has been found to have a positive effect on digestive health, which is attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, thereby reducing inflammation, alleviating symptoms of digestive disorders, and promoting the growth of beneficial gut microbiota.

This plant has been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds that are capable of stimulating the production of white blood cells, activating immune cells, and increasing the production of cytokines, thereby boosting the immune system and reducing the risk of infections.

This plant has been found to have hepatoprotective effects, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds that are capable of reducing oxidative stress, inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, and promoting the growth of beneficial gut microbiota, thereby protecting liver function and alleviating symptoms of liver diseases.

This plant has been found to have a positive effect on respiratory health, which is attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, thereby reducing inflammation, alleviating symptoms of respiratory disorders, and promoting the growth of beneficial lung microbiota.

This plant has been found to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health, which is attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds that have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antihypertensive effects, thereby reducing oxidative stress, alleviating symptoms of cardiovascular disorders, and promoting the growth of beneficial cardiovascular microbiota.

benefits_description: Angelica sinensis antioxidant properties protect against oxidative stress and cell damage by neutralizing free radicals, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation, which is responsible for various diseases, including cardiovascular conditions. The reduction of inflammation also lowers blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis improves cognitive function by enhancing brain-derived neurotrophic factor and promoting neuroprotection, which helps alleviate symptoms of neurodegenerative diseases. In addition to these benefits, Angelica sinensis relieves menstrual pain by inhibiting prostaglandin production, supporting digestive health by stimulating digestive enzymes, and boosting immune system by increasing antibody production. The plant also protects liver function by reducing liver damage, eases respiratory issues by relaxing bronchial smooth muscles, and regulates cardiovascular health by improving lipid profiles and reducing platelet aggregation.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Angelica sinensis improves blood flow by stimulating blood vessel dilation due to its vasodilatory properties, while also enhancing circulation by increasing the production of nitric oxide, a potent vasodilator. Reducing blood pressure is another effect of Angelica sinensis, attributed to its antioxidant properties, which counteract oxidative stress and endothelial dysfunction. The plant boosts red blood cells by increasing erythropoietin production, a hormone that regulates red blood cell formation, ultimately improving oxygen delivery to tissues. Additionally, Angelica sinensis prevents blood clots by inhibiting platelet aggregation and adhesion, while protecting blood vessels from oxidative damage and inflammation. Furthermore, it regulates heart rate by modulating the autonomic nervous system, stimulates collagen production, and reduces inflammation in blood vessels, ultimately protecting them from damage and maintaining cardiovascular health.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Angelica sinensis stimulates digestion by increasing the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which enhances the breakdown of nutrients. It soothes intestinal issues through its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria and reduce inflammation in the intestinal lining. The anti-inflammatory properties of Angelica sinensis also reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making it an effective treatment for conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome. Furthermore, it improves absorption by increasing the surface area of the intestinal lining and promoting the absorption of nutrients. Additionally, it relaxes gut muscles, prevents diarrhea, cures constipation, boosts gut motility, heals gut lining, and protects gut flora, resulting in a balanced and healthy digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Angelica sinensis regulates adrenal function by exerting its adaptogenic properties, allowing the body to adapt to stress and maintain homeostasis. This adaptogenic effect also supports thyroid balance by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-thyroid axis, thereby ensuring proper thyroid hormone production and secretion. The antioxidant properties of Angelica sinensis reduce cortisol levels by scavenging free radicals that contribute to cortisol production, thereby promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Additionally, Angelica sinensis improves insulin sensitivity by inhibiting the activity of phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase, a key enzyme involved in insulin signaling, thereby facilitating glucose uptake in cells and regulating blood sugar levels. As a result, Angelica sinensis boosts metabolic rate, stabilizes blood sugar, activates the pituitary gland, regenerates pancreatic cells, enhances adrenal adaptation, and normalizes hormonal levels, ultimately maintaining endocrine system balance and function.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Angelica sinensis protects skin by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that break down collagen and elastin, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce inflammation, improve moisture, and regenerate tissue. It enhances elasticity by stimulating the production of collagen and elastin, while its antimicrobial properties soothe irritation and fight infections, thereby preventing aging and promoting healthy skin. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators, which in turn relaxes muscles and stimulates healing. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also improve moisture and soothe irritation, allowing the skin to regenerate tissue and repair itself. By reducing inflammation and stimulating healing, Angelica sinensis enhances the overall health of the integumentary system, promoting a youthful and radiant appearance.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Angelica sinensis boosts lymph flow by containing coumarins, which exhibit vasodilatory properties that increase blood and lymph circulation. Stimulating lymph nodes is attributed to its sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to enhance immune response and promote lymph node function. This herb enhances lymph drainage due to its diuretic properties, which increase urine production and promote the removal of toxins from the body. Angelica sinensis reduces lymph swelling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which decrease swelling and pain associated with lymph-related conditions. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Angelica sinensis improve lymph circulation by protecting lymph vessels from oxidative damage and inflammation, while its antimicrobial properties enhance lymphatic filtration by preventing the growth of pathogenic microorganisms in the lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Angelica sinensis reduce joint pain by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, while also displaying antioxidant activity that shields joints from oxidative stress. Ease muscle tension is facilitated by the plant's ability to relax muscle spasms through its sedative and muscle-relaxant properties. Relieve nerve pain is achieved due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce nerve inflammation and block pain signals. Improve bone density is promoted by the plant's ability to regulate osteoclast activity and enhance osteoblast function, leading to increased bone formation and reduced bone resorption. Angelica sinensis also stabilize ligament and enhance cartilage growth by modulating collagen synthesis, regulate joint inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, protect tendon damage by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, soothe muscle spasms through its muscle-relaxant properties, promote bone healing by regulating osteoclast and osteoblast activity, and finally, enhance cartilage growth by stimulating collagen synthesis and inhibiting matrix metalloproteinases.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Angelica sinensis calms anxiety by possessing anti-anxiety and sedative compounds like bergapten and isoimperatorin. Reducing stress is achieved through its adaptogenic properties, which help regulate the body's response to stress, mitigating the effects of cortisol. Soothing nerves is facilitated by its ability to inhibit the reuptake of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, promoting a sense of calm. Improving sleep quality is attributed to its sedative properties, which also enhance the sleep-promoting effects of GABA. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids also enhance focus by improving cognitive function, boost alertness through its stimulatory effects on neurotransmitters, regulate mood by influencing serotonin levels, relax muscles by inhibiting muscle spasms, alleviate pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain pathways, and protect brain cells through antioxidant activity.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Angelica sinensis stimulates ovulation by containing bioactive compounds that regulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, a key process in the reproductive system. This plant regulates menstrual cycles through its antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infections that can disrupt the menstrual cycle. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory properties improve fertility in both men and women, as it reduces oxidative stress and inflammation in the reproductive tract. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Angelica sinensis enhance libido by promoting the production of sex hormones and reducing stress-induced hormonal imbalances. By supporting hormonal balance, regulating menstrual cycles, and improving fertility, Angelica sinensis promotes conception, reduces infertility, soothes endometriosis, cures menopausal symptoms, and increases fecundity, ultimately ensuring a healthy reproductive system.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Angelica sinensis prevents inflammation by utilizing its anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of volatile oils and sesquiterpenes. Its ability to reduce bronchitis is due to the antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria and fungi, thereby preventing respiratory infections. The plant eases congestion by possessing expectorant properties that facilitate the removal of mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Angelica sinensis also treats asthma by relaxing airways with its antispasmodic properties, allowing for improved airflow and reduced symptoms. Additionally, its antioxidant properties fight infections, alleviate wheezing, relieve coughing, soothe irritation, and prevent wheezing, making it a comprehensive treatment for respiratory issues.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Angelica sinensis relieves bladder issues through its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. It improves urination by stimulating the kidneys and promoting the expulsion of urine due to its diuretic and antispasmodic effects. The antimicrobial properties of Angelica sinensis prevent infection by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms in the urinary tract. It reduces frequency by regulating the balance of kidney function and promoting the efficient elimination of waste products, thus alleviating the sensation of urgency. Additionally, Angelica sinensis cures cystitis, heals prostatitis, alleviates incontinence, treats renal issues, protects kidneys, and soothes irritation in the urinary system through a combination of its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.

properties_list.description: Angelica sinensis has been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity, primarily through its ability to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms such as bacteria and fungi, thereby preventing infections and promoting wound healing.

The plant has been reported to possess antioxidant properties, which help in neutralizing free radicals and protecting cells from oxidative stress, thus preventing cell damage and promoting overall health.

Angelica sinensis has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which helps in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis, thereby promoting healing and reducing pain.
The plant has been reported to possess analgesic properties, which help in reducing pain and discomfort associated with various conditions, thereby promoting relief and improving quality of life.

Angelica sinensis has been found to exhibit antipyretic activity, which helps in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms associated with conditions such as influenza, thereby promoting recovery and reducing discomfort.

The plant has been reported to possess hepatoprotective properties, which help in protecting the liver from damage caused by toxins and other harmful substances, thereby promoting liver health and function.

Angelica sinensis has been found to exhibit cardioprotective activity, which helps in protecting the heart from damage caused by factors such as high blood pressure and atherosclerosis, thereby promoting cardiovascular health and reducing the risk of heart disease.

The plant has been reported to possess neuroprotective properties, which help in protecting the brain and nervous system from damage caused by factors such as Alzheimer's disease and Parkinson's disease, thereby promoting cognitive function and reducing the risk of neurodegenerative disorders.

Angelica sinensis has been found to exhibit cytotoxic activity, which helps in inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis, thereby exerting anti-cancer effects and promoting tumor regression.

The plant has been reported to possess immunomodulatory properties, which help in regulating and enhancing the immune system's response to pathogens and other foreign substances, thereby promoting immune function and reducing the risk of infections.

parts_description: The most used parts of Angelica sinensis for medicinal purposes are roots, rhizomes, and leaves. The roots contain primary bioactive compounds such as ferulic acid, isoflavones, and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties. The rhizomes contain volatile oils, including ligustilide and butylphthalide, which display anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and sedative effects. The leaves contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-viral properties. These bioactive compounds in the roots, rhizomes, and leaves of Angelica sinensis contribute to its traditional medicinal uses for conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory disorders.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain major bioactive compounds including ferulic acid, hesperidin, and polysaccharides, and exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, primarily offering cardiovascular protection, anti-cancer effects, and improved immune function.

parts_list.name: rhizomes
parts_list.description: The rhizomes of this plant contain significant amounts of bioactive compounds including ferulic acid, angelan, and ligustilide, and display anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties, mainly resulting in analgesic, anti-anxiety, and anti-convulsant effects, as well as improved cardiovascular health and reduced inflammation.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain major bioactive compounds including ferulic acid, hesperidin, and flavonoids, and exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, primarily offering anti-cancer effects, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced immune function, as well as anti-viral and anti-bacterial activity.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat menstrual disorders, promote digestion, improve blood circulation and relieve fever, as well as reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions.
preparations.preparation_overview: Angelica sinensis capsule is a widely used herbal preparation made from the roots and rhizomes of the Angelica sinensis plant. It's commonly used to treat menstrual disorders, menopause symptoms, fatigue, hypertension, digestive issues, anemia, kidney disease, and certain types of cancer including leukemia and some types of lymphoma. The capsule is particularly popular due to its widespread availability and frequent recommendation by traditional Chinese medicine practitioners. One of the most notable effects of Angelica sinensis capsule is its ability to reduce blood pressure and improve circulation. The preparation is made by harvesting the roots and rhizomes of the plant, which are then dried and processed into a capsule form. This process is moderately difficult due to the need for precise handling and storage of the plant material to preserve its medicinal properties.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes, and dry them thoroughly to remove excess moisture.
Cut the dried Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes into smaller pieces to increase their surface area.
Grind the cut Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground Angelica sinensis powder with a suitable binder and excipient to form a uniform blend.
Fill the uniform blend into empty capsule shells, and seal them securely to produce the Angelica sinensis herbal capsules.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat menstrual disorders, anemia, high blood pressure and as an antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent.
preparations.preparation_overview: Angelica sinensis tincture is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat a range of health issues including cough, menstrual disorders, digestive issues, fatigue, sleep disorders, hypertension, anxiety, inflammation and cardiovascular disease. It's widely used due to its effectiveness and is often found in traditional Chinese medicine. The effects of Angelica sinensis tincture include reducing inflammation, lowering blood pressure and improving sleep quality. This preparation is made from the roots and rhizomes of the Angelica sinensis plant, which are rich in compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide. The process of creating Angelica sinensis tincture involves maceration or percolation, which can be moderately difficult to do correctly, requiring precise measurement and timing to ensure the desired potency.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean a sufficient quantity of Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes.
Dry the roots and rhizomes in a clean, well-ventilated area to prevent mold and bacterial growth.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered roots and rhizomes with a neutral solvent such as vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for several weeks, shaking the jar regularly to facilitate extraction.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat menstrual disorders, anemia, and as a general tonic to improve blood circulation and overall health.
preparations.preparation_overview: Angelica sinensis decoction is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various health conditions, including menstrual irregularity, anemia, hepatitis, migraine, urinary tract infection, respiratory infection, and gastrointestinal disorder. It is a common preparation, widely used in traditional Chinese medicine. The decoction has a common effect of increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation. It is typically made with the roots and rhizomes of the Angelica sinensis plant. The preparation is moderately difficult to make, requiring careful measurement and cooking of the plant material to extract its active compounds.
preparation_recipe: Gather clean and dried Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes.
Rinse the Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes with cold water to remove any impurities.
Combine the cleaned Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes in a pot and add enough water to cover them.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat menstrual disorders, relieve menopausal symptoms, and improve blood circulation.
preparations.preparation_overview: Angelica sinensis tea is used to treat menstrual issues, including dysmenorrhea and amenorrhea, by reducing uterine contractions and improving blood flow. It's also used to alleviate fatigue, anxiety, insomnia, digestive issues, hypertension, respiratory issues, and inflammation due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. This preparation is extremely common, particularly in Asian cultures, where it's been used for centuries to promote overall health and well-being. One of its most common effects is improving sleep quality, which is often attributed to its sedative properties. Angelica sinensis tea is made from the dried roots and rhizomes of the Angelica sinensis plant, which are typically combined with other herbs to create a soothing and relaxing brew. Making this tea is relatively easy, as it requires steeping the dried roots and rhizomes in hot water for 5-10 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse and release their medicinal properties.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the roots and rhizomes of the Angelica sinensis plant.
Dry the roots and rhizomes in a well-ventilated area to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered roots and rhizomes with boiling water in a teapot to create the tea.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes, then strain and serve as a herbal tea.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat menstrual irregularities, reduce fever, alleviate cough, and support cardiovascular health
preparations.preparation_overview: Angelica sinensis salve is a traditional herbal preparation used to treat various conditions, including menstrual cramps, rheumatism, skin ulcers, bruises, wounds, varicose veins, fungal infections and burns. It's a common remedy in many cultures, often used by practitioners of traditional medicine. The preparation has a well-documented anti-inflammatory effect. Angelica sinensis salve is typically made from the roots and rhizomes of the Angelica plant, which are rich in bioactive compounds. The process of making the salve is moderately complex, requiring some knowledge of herbalism and preparation techniques, but the end result is a potent topical treatment for a range of skin and musculoskeletal issues.
preparation_recipe: Gather Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes, clean, and dry them thoroughly.
Chop the roots and rhizomes into small pieces and combine them in a pot with a carrier oil such as olive or jojoba oil, and simmer them over low heat for several hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids, and let the liquid cool to room temperature.
Add a beeswax and emulsifier mixture to the cooled liquid and heat the mixture gently, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Pour the herbal salve mixture into small containers, let it cool and solidify, and seal the containers to store the salve.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Angelica sinensis are abdominal pain, allergic reactions can manifest in various forms including skin rashes and difficulty breathing, arrhythmia may occur due to the plant's potential to interact with cardiac medications, bradycardia or a slower heart rate can be a result of its influence on the autonomic nervous system, cough and diarrhea can be exacerbated in individuals with pre-existing respiratory and gastrointestinal conditions, headache is a common symptom, hypotension or low blood pressure may be experienced, nausea is a frequent complaint, and vomiting can be a severe and potentially life-threatening complication.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Prolonged use of Angelica sinensis may stimulate uterine contractions.

precautions.precaution_name: Use with caution in bleeding disorders
precautions.precaution_list_description: May exacerbate bleeding disorders such as hemophilia.
Angelica sinensis may interact with anticoagulant medications, such as warfarin.
Hypertension may be exacerbated by Angelica sinensis due to its diuretic and vasodilatory effects.

precautions.precaution_name: Use with caution in kidney or liver disease
precautions.precaution_list_description: Avoid use in individuals with kidney or liver disease due to potential exacerbation of conditions.
Combining Angelica sinensis with anticoagulant medications increases the risk of bleeding.
Use with caution in pediatric populations due to potential toxicity and adverse effects.

Contraindicated in pregnancy and breastfeeding, and used in conjunction with conventional treatment under medical guidance due to potential interactions with anticoagulant and hypoglycemic medications.
Allergic reactions occur due to contact with Angelica sinensis.
Gastrointestinal upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions, including skin rash, occur.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Angelica sinensis has been found to have various positive effects, including anti-inflammatory activity, antifibrotic action, and antioxidant activities. Additionally, it has been shown to possess antispasmodic activity, neuroprotective action, and cardio- and cerebrovascular effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. Furthermore, the study suggests that Angelica sinensis may also have therapeutic potentials in treating female irregular menstruation and amenorrhea.

According to a study published by "Zhongguo Zhong yao za zhi = Zhongguo zhongyao zazhi = China journal of Chinese materia medica", Angelica sinensis has been found to possess various bioactive compounds, including (Z)-ligustilide, (Z)-6,7-epoxyligustilide, and (Z)-6,7-cis-dihydroxyligustilide, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. These compounds have been isolated from the roots of Angelica sinensis through chromatographic and spectroscopic methods. The presence of these compounds suggests that Angelica sinensis may have potential health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.

According to a study published by "Current medicinal chemistry", Angelica sinensis has been found to possess various bioactivities, including antimicrobial, anticancer, antitumour, analgesic, and anti-inflammatory properties. This has led to its traditional use as a remedy for various health conditions, such as arthritis, indigestion, and chronic bronchitis. Additionally, the study suggests that Angelica sinensis may also have hepatoprotective and nephroprotective effects, making it a potential candidate for the development of new medications.

According to a study published by "Nutrients", the root water extract of Angelica sinensis has been found to exhibit significant anti-inflammatory effects. The extract was shown to inhibit the production of various pro-inflammatory cytokines, including interleukin-6 and tumor necrosis factor-alpha, and reduce the expression of inflammatory genes. Additionally, Angelica sinensis root water extract was found to restore cell viability and reduce intracellular calcium levels in LPS-induced RAW 264.7 mouse macrophages.

According to a study published by "Yao xue xue bao = Acta pharmaceutica Sinica", the polysaccharide fraction of Angelica sinensis has been found to have a positive effect on the immune system. It was discovered that this fraction, known as AP-3, significantly enhanced the percentage of CD4+ lymphocytes in total splenocytes, and augmented the production of IL-2 in a time and dose-dependent manner. Additionally, AP-3 was found to upregulate IL-2 and IFN-gamma expression, thereby regulating the immune response and activating Th1 cells.

According to a study published by "Frontiers in pharmacology", Angelica sinensis has been shown to possess effective analgesic and anti-inflammatory actions, making it a beneficial herb for individuals suffering from pain and inflammation. The extracts and compounds isolated from Angelica sinensis have also demonstrated positive effects on the central nervous system, cardiovascular system, and exhibited deworming activity. These findings suggest that Angelica sinensis may be a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions, warranting further research in the fields of pharmacology and toxicology.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Angelica sinensis are attributed to its ability to exhibit antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms, while its antioxidant properties play a crucial role in scavenging free radicals and preventing oxidative stress. Angelica sinensis also possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which help to mitigate inflammatory responses and alleviate associated symptoms, and its analgesic properties enable it to relieve pain by inhibiting the production of pain mediators. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis has been found to possess antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pyrogenic substances, and it also exhibits hepatoprotective effects, protecting the liver from damage caused by toxins and free radicals. Additionally, Angelica sinensis has cardioprotective effects, helping to prevent cardiovascular diseases by improving lipid profiles and preventing vascular dysfunction, and it also possesses neuroprotective properties, which help to protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation. The bioactive compounds responsible for these medicinal properties include flavonoids, saponins, and volatile oils, such as ferulic acid, ligustilide, and butylphthalide, which have been found to possess immunomodulatory and cytotoxic effects, helping to regulate the immune system and induce apoptosis in cancer cells.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Angelica sinensis are primarily responsible for its medicinal properties, with phenolic acids such as ferulic and sinapic acids exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to its cardiovascular and neuroprotective effects. Saponins, including aciguette and acilin, are known for their hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory actions, which may help mitigate liver damage and inflammation. Coumarins, including scopoletin and umbelliferone, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties, contributing to the herb's cardiovascular benefits. Flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, are involved in its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help protect against cell damage and inflammation. Ligustilide, a major volatile oil component, has been demonstrated to possess anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and vasodilatory effects, contributing to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Angelica sinensis for medicinal purposes are capsules, which contain dried and powdered roots. The dried roots are also used to make a tincture, an alcohol-based extract used to treat menstrual cramps and other symptoms of premenstrual syndrome. A decoction, a liquid extract made by boiling the roots in water, is used to treat various conditions including fever, rheumatism, and indigestion. The dried roots can also be steeped in hot water to make a tea, which is used to treat conditions such as insomnia, anemia, and high blood pressure. In addition, a salve, a topical ointment made from the roots, is used to treat skin conditions including eczema and dermatitis.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Angelica sinensis medicinally are to avoid its use in pregnancy and breastfeeding due to potential hormonal effects on the fetus or infant, use with caution in bleeding disorders such as hemophilia as it may exacerbate bleeding, may interact with blood thinners by increasing their efficacy, monitor blood pressure as it can cause hypotension, use with caution in kidney or liver disease as it may worsen liver function, may exacerbate bleeding with anticoagulants by increasing the risk of hemorrhage, avoid its use in children and infants as their metabolism and excretion systems are still developing, use under medical supervision to minimize potential risks, may cause allergic reactions such as skin rashes or anaphylaxis, and monitor for side effects such as dizziness, nausea, or diarrhea.
